Arc 2a: ordinary mu random intercept (1|id) via ML-Laplace. DG2 sentinel (test-arc2a-mu-random-intercept.R / test-tweedie-location-scale.R): convergence=0, pdHess=TRUE, sd estimate within 0.30 of truth, BLUP vs true-effect correlation >0.45 (>0.40 ordinal), profile_targets() profile_ready=TRUE for sd:mu:(1|id), and check_drm() mu_random_effect_replication=ok. Intercept-only; random slopes, sigma/shape/inflation-dpar REs, and labelled covariance blocks are rejected. Small-cluster RE-SD may be biased low under the Laplace approximation (AGHQ/REML remedies planned) and there is no coverage study -> point_fit_recovery, not inference_ready/supported.

Arc 4c promotes only the standard ML-Laplace profile interval for the natural-scale SD of one ordinary independent mu slope (0 + x | id), true SD 0.50, the frozen zero-one beta DGP, and M>=16. ML-Laplace profile coverage for sd:mu:(0 + x | id), true SD 0.50, n_each=15, interior precision 6.25, labelled 15% balanced structural-boundary allocation, N=1200/M: M16 0.9292 (exact 95% CI 0.9132-0.9430), M32 0.9400 (0.9250-0.9528), M64 0.9517 (0.9380-0.9631); availability 1.000. Certified floor M=16; inference_ready_with_caveats only, not supported. Interior rbeta draws produced 50, 87, and 193 machine-exact ones across all M16, M32, and M64 replicates, so the fitted data had slightly more than 15% observed boundary mass. Noether WITHHOLD; Fisher and Rose PROMOTE, so the frozen two-WITHHOLD block was not met. Campaign sd_hat and Wald diagnostics are unavailable; no bias or Wald claim is made. Excludes other SD values, observation counts, group grids, fixtures, random intercepts, correlated or labelled slopes, scale-side random effects, structured effects, other families, REML, and AGHQ/O3.
